title: Twelve Experiences
content: I do this thing every December. Write down twelve specific experiences for the next year. Not vague goals - experiences. 'Take a pottery class' not 'learn something new.' 'Weekend camping trip with kids' not 'spend more family time.' Last year I hit ten of twelve. Specificity makes it real.
extended_content: Vague goals die. 'Travel more' doesn't happen. 'Book a weekend trip to Portland in March' happens. 'Get healthier' is a wish. 'Sign up for the 5K in June' is a plan. The twelve experiences list works because each one is a thing you can actually DO, with a time you can actually do it. By the end of the year, you don't have vague regrets. You have specific memories. Or specific lessons about why it didn't happen.
